experiment: nano moediag 200-iter run from iter_7000 ckpt, new per-iter tokens_per_expert logging

loss_delta.note: Matches the 7485-step full-retrain last-100-mean Δ = +0.00546 almost exactly.

interpretation: nano routes tokens 3.6x MORE UNIFORMLY than ref. ref's max expert gets ~3x its fair share of tokens; nano's max expert gets only ~1.5x.

ref and nano use mathematically identical aux-free bias correction: bias += sign(mean - counts) * 0.001 per optim step, with all-reduce across DP. Yet steady-state routing distribution is very different.
This is the most likely source of the stable +0.005 nat gap. Over-uniform routing reduces expert specialization; specialized experts produce lower loss on their preferred tokens.

Next step: determine whether nano's over-uniformity comes from (a) more effective bias accumulation path, (b) different topk tie-breaking in bf16, (c) scale-invariance differences in sign() updates when counts are aggregated differently.
